Member-only story

How Sakana Fugu and Claude Fable represent two competing paths for frontier AI: orchestration, long-context reasoning, and enterprise control.

10 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Monolithic stability vs. Agentic orchestration: The architectural trade-off between a single point of failure and decentralized latency.
At first glance, Sakana Fugu and Claude Fable look like two entries in the same market. In reality, they represent two very different answers to the same question: should frontier capability come from a monolithic reasoning engine, or from a system that knows how to coordinate many models at once?
That distinction may end up defining the next phase of AI more than raw model IQ.
The Old Assumption
For most of the current AI cycle, we all operated on one dominant assumption: the future belongs to the single most powerful model. I used to believe this too. As someone who is deploying pipelines on AWS and trying to version chaos in DagsHub, the dream was always “one model to rule them all.” Just give me the smartest API endpoint and let me sleep. But real-world MLOps taught me that monolithic intelligence is a nightmare when things break
Claude Fable fits that worldview almost perfectly.
